evals.prompt: Audit our AWS costs: we're spending $45k/month. We have 20 m5.xlarge EC2 instances running 24/7 (average CPU 15%), 50TB in S3 Standard (80% hasn't been accessed in 6 months), RDS db.r5.2xlarge at 30% utilization, and everything is on-demand pricing. No Savings Plans.
evals.expected_output: A cost optimization audit identifying right-sizing opportunities, storage tiering savings, and pricing model recommendations with estimated savings.
assertions: Identifies EC2 instances as significantly over-provisioned (15% CPU)
Recommends right-sizing or smaller instance types
Recommends S3 Intelligent-Tiering or Glacier for cold data
Identifies RDS as over-provisioned and recommends downsizing
Recommends Savings Plans or Reserved Instances for steady-state workloads
Provides estimated dollar savings for key recommendations

evals.prompt: we're getting killed on data transfer costs — $8k/month just on cross-region transfers and NAT gateway. We have services in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 that talk to each other constantly. Also spending $3k/month on CloudWatch logs we never look at.
evals.expected_output: A focused cost audit addressing data transfer patterns and logging costs with architecture-level recommendations (not just 'turn things off').
assertions: Addresses cross-region data transfer with architecture recommendations
Suggests reducing NAT gateway costs (VPC endpoints, architecture changes)
Recommends log retention policies or tiering for CloudWatch
Provides specific alternatives (e.g., VPC endpoints, caching, regional placement)
Does not just recommend 'delete everything' but offers architectural solutions

evals.prompt: cost review: serverless app with Lambda (2M invocations/month, 512MB, avg 200ms), DynamoDB on-demand (unpredictable traffic, spiky), API Gateway REST API, CloudFront. Monthly bill is $2k which seems high for the traffic.
evals.expected_output: An audit assessing whether serverless costs are reasonable for the workload, identifying specific optimization opportunities within the serverless model.
assertions: Evaluates whether Lambda memory/duration is optimized (power tuning)
Assesses DynamoDB on-demand vs provisioned trade-off given the traffic pattern
Considers API Gateway pricing alternatives (HTTP API vs REST API)
Does not recommend moving away from serverless without justification
Provides context on whether $2k is reasonable for the described workload
